channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iraq's top court throws out challenge to new laws, including one increasing Islamic courts authority
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — Iraq’s top court on Tuesday threw out a legal challenge that had temporarily halted three controversial laws passed last month by the country’s parliament.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Families bid farewell to victims of Guatemala bus crash
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO LOS OCOTES, Guatemala (AP) — Hours before dawn, Julio Arrivillaga and Catalina Pérez Molina boarded a bus with other residents in the center of this humble village for what should have been an hour-long ride to Guatemala’s capital.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau calls out 'unjustified' Trump tariffs on steel, aluminum
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau called President Trump’s tariffs on steel and aluminum imports “entirely unjustified” and said the country would respond “if it comes to that.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Jordan, Arab nations oppose Palestinian displacement, Abdullah says after Trump meeting
item.description: King Abdullah II of Jordan said Tuesday that Arab nations are unified in their opposition to displacing Palestinians from Gaza, following a meeting with President Trump in which the president reiterated his plans for Palestinians to move elsewhere while the U.S. rebuilds Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis accepts the resignation of a Peruvian bishop accused of ignoring abuse
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Pope Francis on Tuesday accepted the resignation of an influential Peruvian bishop, who has come under criticism for not doing enough to stop abuses committed by a conservative Catholic movement that was recently disbanded by the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel’s fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman puts the focus on West Bank violence
item.description: KAFR AL-LABAD, West Bank (AP) — The call came in the middle of the night, Mohammed Shula said. His daughter-in-law, eight months pregnant with her first child, was whispering. There was panic in her voice. “Help, please,” Shula recalled her saying. “You have to save us.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of Hamas hostage release deadline
item.description: Israel is amassing troops in and around the Gaza Strip ahead of the upcoming Hamas hostage release, which the Palestinian militant group threatened to delay after accusing Israel of violating the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump secures release of American detained in Russia
item.description: The Trump administration announced Tuesday it has negotiated the release of Marc Fogel, an American schoolteacher who has been detained in Russia on drug charges since his arrest in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: American Marc Fogel released from Russian custody
item.description: An American teacher detained by Russia is heading back to American soil, the Trump administration announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO allies await US defense secretary's visit, the first by a member of the Trump administration
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will on Wednesday become the first member of the new Trump administration to visit NATO, where the allies are keen to understand how America now plans to influence the course of the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House fires USAID inspector general after warning about funding oversight, officials say
item.description: The White House fired the inspector general for the U.S. Agency for International Development on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli cabinet backs Trump’s demand for Hamas to release all hostages by Saturday deadline
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet supports President Trump’s demand that Hamas release all of its hostages by noon on Saturday, an Israeli official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance, Bessent to meet with Zelensky amid Trump's push to end the war in Ukraine
item.description: Vice President Vance and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent are set to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky amid the administration’s push for the war with Russia to end.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky proposes swap of seized territory with Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is proposing an exchange of seized territory with Russia as part of any potential negotiations to end the war. Ukraine holds control of a small pocket of Russian territory, parts of Kursk Oblast, captured during a surprise incursion in August.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump remains committed to US owning Gaza, says Middle East states could help rebuild war-torn area
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is committed to the U.S. purchasing and owning Gaza, but that he may allow Middle Eastern countries to rebuild sections of the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer are leading early presidential voting results
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador is likely to choose its next president in a runoff election later this year as early results of a first-round vote Sunday showed a tight difference between conservative President Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian protesters block key bridge, roads to mark 100 days since deadly canopy collapse
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Striking university students in Serbia blocked a bridge in Belgrade as well as roads throughout the Balkan country on Sunday to mark 100 days since the collapse of a concrete canopy at a train station which killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marine identified as victim of surveillance flight that crashed in Philippines
item.description: Military officials identified a U.S. Marine who died in a plane crash in the Philippines last week as Sgt. Jacob M. Durham, a California native.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare wildlife spotting caught on camera in Poland
item.description: A wildlife conservation group in Poland was conducting research on beavers when they spotted two rare black wolves on video footage captured in a forest last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, South Africa in growing row over hotly contested land law, country's deals with US foes
item.description: Trump’s executive order cutting aid and offering refugee status to South Africa’s Afrikaner farmers has caused a stir in the African country after passage of a controversial land bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Parents of American murdered by Hamas make 'plea' to Trump after latest hostage release
item.description: The parents of murdered Israeli-American hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin pleaded with President Donald Trump to get the remaining 76 hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Days before Trump halted funding, an ex-Israeli hostage was held at UNRWA school in Gaza, she reveals
item.description: Former Israeli hostage Emily Damari said she was held in an UNRWA center in Gaza. Damari was recently released as part of the hostages for ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New report by panel of experts claims killer nurse Lucy Letby is innocent
item.description: Convicted of killing seven babies and attempting to kill seven more, Lucy Letby's conviction is being questioned by a new report that claims she is a victim of a miscarriage of justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu: 'No future' in Gaza unless 'Hamas destroyed' amid concern for hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Hamas must be 'destroyed,' following discussions with President Trump and lawmakers on the Hill, despite concerns that hard rhetoric could jeopardize hostage releases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation
